properties.name: User Identity
properties.description: A unique identifier representing a person within an enterprise system. May take different forms across systems: numeric IDs, email addresses, employee codes, or worker IDs. The same physical person may have different identifiers in different systems.

properties.name: Event Timestamp
properties.description: The date and time when an event occurred. Different systems use different timestamp formats: MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, ISO 8601, or Unix epochs. All represent the same concept of when something happened.

properties.name: Organizational Unit
properties.description: The department, business unit, or organizational group that a person belongs to. Different systems may use different labels (department, business_unit, org_unit) and slightly different naming conventions for the same groups.

properties.name: Workstation / Computer
properties.description: A physical or virtual computer, workstation, or endpoint used by an employee. Identified by a machine name or asset tag. The same computer may appear across authentication, browsing, and email logs.

properties.name: Authentication Event
properties.description: A logon or logoff event where a user establishes or terminates a session on a computer or system. Indicates who accessed what system and when.

properties.name: Web Browsing Activity
properties.description: A record of a user accessing a website or web resource. Includes the URL visited, content type, and data transferred. Reveals internet usage patterns and potential policy violations.

properties.name: Email Communication
properties.description: An email message sent by a user including metadata about recipients, size, and attachments. Reveals communication patterns, external contacts, and potential data exfiltration indicators.

properties.name: Call / Meeting Duration
properties.description: The length of time a video call, audio call, or virtual meeting lasted. Different platforms use different field names (duration_minutes, length_minutes) but represent the same measurement.

properties.name: Meeting Participant Count
properties.description: The number of people who participated in a call or meeting. Some systems store this as a count (participant_count), others as an array of attendees whose length gives the count.

properties.name: Meeting Organizer / Host
properties.description: The person who created or initiated a video call or meeting. Called 'organizer' in Teams and 'host' in Zoom, but represents the same role.

properties.name: Call / Meeting Type
properties.description: The classification of a call or meeting. Teams uses audio/video/screen_share to describe the media type. Zoom uses instant/scheduled/recurring to describe how it was organized. Related but different categorization schemes.

properties.name: Employee Name
properties.description: The full name of an employee or worker. Different systems use different field names (name, full_name) but contain the same information.

properties.name: Job Title / Position
properties.description: The role or position title held by an employee. Different HR systems may use 'job_role' or 'position_title' but represent the same concept.

properties.name: Compensation / Salary
properties.description: The annual base pay of an employee. May be labeled 'salary' or 'base_compensation' in different systems. Represents the same concept of how much the employee is paid.

properties.name: Performance Rating
properties.description: An assessment of an employee's job performance. The legacy system uses an integer 1-5 scale (performance_rating), while the modern system uses a continuous 0.0-5.0 scale (performance_score). Both measure the same underlying concept but with different precision.

properties.name: Employment Period
properties.description: The time span of an employee's tenure, defined by their hire/start date and optional termination/end date. Different systems use different field names (hire_date/termination_date vs start_date/end_date).

properties.name: Ticket Workflow Status
properties.description: The current state of a project ticket in its lifecycle: Open (new), In Progress (being worked on), Done (work finished), or Closed (verified complete).

properties.name: Ticket Assignee
properties.description: The person responsible for completing work on a project ticket. Identified by email address, which can be linked to employee records in HR systems.

properties.name: Effort Estimate
properties.description: The estimated work effort for a project ticket, measured in story points using the Fibonacci scale (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13). Higher values indicate more complex or time-consuming work.

properties.name: Data Transfer Volume
properties.description: The amount of data transferred in an event, measured in bytes. Appears in HTTP access logs (bytes_transferred) and email records (size_bytes). Relevant for detecting data exfiltration.
